title: Adding $500 to These 3 Stocks Would Be a Genius Move Right Now
text: Lots of insiders are stepping into Charles Schwab following the stock's unjustified stumble. Bank of America has been upended by the fallout from the Silicon Valley Bank debacle.

title: The Best Dividend Stocks to Buy Right Now Could Be Hiding in Plain Sight
text: Verizon may not see much growth ahead, but it's going to be a terrific cash cow for a long time. Few other companies are positioned as well as Coca-Cola to maintain their dividend growth streak.

title: Prudential Financial Could Be Right For Your Long-Term Portfolio
text: Prudential remains a blue-chip insurance stock. Management has made strides to improve both revenue and profitability.

title: Prudential Financial: Price Slump And Dividend Increase Make This High-Yielder Attractive
text: PRU has seen its shares come under pressure, but the underlying performance was attractive. The company offers a high dividend yield of 6% at current prices.

title: Here's Why You Should Hold Prudential Financial (PRU) Stock
text: Prudential Financial (PRU) benefits from higher premiums, strategic acquisitions, favorable underwriting and effective capital deployment.

title: My Dividend Growth Portfolio February Update: Cash Is Not Trash
text: Cash is yielding over 4%, so a dividend growth investor can afford to be patient. February marked the first monthly decline in projected income since 2016 due to dividend cuts.

title: Prudential Financial: An Undervalued Stock Sending More Cash To Shareholders
text: Prudential Financial's after-tax adjusted operating income per share payout ratio will improve from 50.7% in 2022 to just 40.7% in 2023. The company's after-tax adjusted operating income fell 23.9% year-over-year in the fourth quarter.

title: March's 17 Ideal 2023 Barrons/Bloomberg Dividend Dogs
text: Two Barron's Annual Roundtable, plus one Bloomberg Intelligence article published late January listed 99 stocks of-note for 2023; 78 of those were unduplicated US publicly-listed companies, of which 56 were paid dividends. Barron's two roundtable discussions on January 20 and 27 split 49 picks from 9 panelists in half, whereas Bloomberg detailed all 50 in their January 16 BusinessWeek edition.

title: 5 Top High-Yield Stocks to Sail Through a Choppy March
text: As markets gyrate on inflationary pressure, invest in dividend stocks like Regency Centers (REG), Prudential Financial (PRU), Omnicom Group (OMC), NiSource (NI) & NewJersey Resources (NJR) for a steady income.

title: AM Best Assigns Issue Credit Rating to Prudential Financial, Inc.'s New Junior Subordinated Notes
text: OLDWICK, N.J.--(BUSINESS WIRE)--AM Best has assigned a Long-Term Issue Credit Rating of “bbb” (Good) to the $500 million, 6.75% fixed to fixed reset rate junior subordinated notes, due March 1, 2053, of Prudential Financial, Inc. (Prudential) (Newark, NJ) [NYSE: PRU]. The outlook assigned to this Credit Rating (rating) is stable. All other ratings of Prudential and its subsidiaries are unchanged.

title: 7 Stocks to Protect Against the Massive Inflation Shock of 2023
text: Last year has proven to be one of the most challenging years for the U.S. economy. An inflation shock took hold, driving the annualized inflation rate from 1.4% in January 2021 to 7% to close out the year.
